Unveiling the spatial regulation of tumour progression in ovarian cancer

Sara Palomino -Echeverria1,2,3, Ada Junquera -Mencía1, Matías M. Falco1,4, Wenqing Chen1,4, Tatiana Belova3, Ceren Pajanoja5, Ping -an Hsieh3, Anna Vähärautio1,4,

1: Research Program in Systems Oncology, University of Helsinki, Helsinki, Finland : 2: Institute for Molecular Medicine Finland (FIMM), Helsinki, Finland ; 3: Computational Biology and Systems 
Medicine, Centre for Molecular Medicine Norway (NCMM), University of Oslo (UiO), Oslo, Norway ; 4: Foundation for the Finnish Cancer Institute, Helsinki, Finland, 5: iCAN - Digital Precision 
Cancer Medicine, University of Helsinki, Helsinki, Finland ; 6: Department of Biochemistry and Developmental Biology, University of Helsinki, Helsinki ; Finland, 7: Department of Obstetrics and 
Gynecology , Department of Oncology, Helsinki University Hospital, Helsinki, Finland ;*: These authors contributed equally to this work and share last authorship .
Multi -Omic Experimental Design and Spatial Profiling Pipeline for HGSC Study Cohort and Multimodal Workflow .Fresh tumor tissue 
was collected from a cohort of16 High -Grade Serous Carcinoma 
(HGSC) patients . The cohort comprises patients undergoing both 
Primary (PDS, n=6) and Interval (IDS, n=9) Debulking Surgery,

ed from a cohort of16 High -Grade Serous Carcinoma 
(HGSC) patients . The cohort comprises patients undergoing both 
Primary (PDS, n=6) and Interval (IDS, n=9) Debulking Surgery, 
with a predominance of Homologous Recombination Proficient 
(HRP) tumors, and one deficient Platinum resistant . Samples 
were processed in parallel to enable comprehensive profiling : (1) 
Single -Cell Transcriptomics :Tissue dissociation followed 
byscRNA -seq. (2) Spatial Multi -Omics :Fixed FFPE blocks were 
analyzed using Xenium Immuno -Oncology (custom panel 
of380+50genes) to map gene expression in situ. The same 
tissue sections were subsequently subjected tolow-plex t-
CyCIF (16 protein markers) to spatially integrate proteomic with 
transcriptomic data. (3) Hierarchical integration of Xenium and t-
CyCIF .

1. Hsieh PH, Hsiao RX, Ferenc K, Mathelier A, Burkholz R, Chen CY, Sandve GK, Belova T, Kuijjer ML. (2024 .) CAVACHON : a hierarchical variational autoencoder to integrate multi -modal single -cell data. ArXiv [cs.LG] ar Xiv :2405 .18655 Hierarchical integration of spatial transcriptomics and proteomics could potentially enhance tumor cell resolution
Integrated transcriptomic –proteomic clustering using 
CAVACHON . By applying our hierarchical variational autoencoder 
(CAVACHON)1 to Xenium -derived gene expression and t-CycIF 
protein measurements, we aim to identify integrated clusters from a

applying our hierarchical variational autoencoder 
(CAVACHON)1 to Xenium -derived gene expression and t-CycIF 
protein measurements, we aim to identify integrated clusters from a 
shared latent representation . By conditioning t-CycIF markers on 
Xenium genes, the model would potentially demonstrate robust 
performance even with reduced feature sets, highlighting its ability to 
capture subtle differences driven by post-transcriptional regulation .
